executiveSummary:
**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY: MEDICAID PROGRAM PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS**

National Medicaid enrollment declined across most major states in the 2026-01 reporting period, with 75.3 million total enrollees representing a post-unwinding stabilization pattern. California and Illinois experienced the steepest declines at -6.2% year-over-year, while Michigan recorded the largest drop at -7.6%. Texas maintained relative stability with a -3.1% decline, positioning it better than several peer states including Florida (-4.0%) and Ohio (-4.4%). Only North Carolina bucked the trend with modest growth of +0.5%. Spending patterns reveal significant state variation, with the national per-enrollee average of $2,869 in fiscal year 2024 masking substantial disparities—Pennsylvania spending $4,377 per enrollee compared to Florida's $2,031. Texas spending of $2,988 per enrollee falls slightly above the national average, reflecting a moderate cost structure relative to its enrollment base of 4.0 million beneficiaries.

Quality performance and managed care utilization present mixed signals for program effectiveness. The national quality average of 65.6 out of 100 points highlights persistent performance gaps, with Texas scoring 62 and ranking 30th nationally—placing it in the bottom tier of moderate performers alongside states like Arkansas and South Carolina. However, Texas demonstrates strong managed care penetration at 93.3%, significantly exceeding the national average of 83.2% and covering 3.9 million enrollees. Recent federal regulatory activity indicates heightened oversight, with CMS announcing temporary enrollment moratoria and new HCBS quality measures for 2028, suggesting upcoming policy shifts that may impact state operations and federal matching funds. Leadership should monitor these developments closely, particularly given Texas's moderate quality performance and the potential for enhanced federal scrutiny of underperforming states.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, representing a substantial -3.1% year-over-year decline that aligns closely with the national unwinding trend and mirrors Pennsylvania's identical contraction rate. The state's per-enrollee spending of $2,988 sits modestly above the national average of $2,869 but remains significantly lower than expansion states like New York ($3,431) and Pennsylvania ($4,377), reflecting Texas's restrictive eligibility criteria as a non-expansion state. Notably, Texas achieves remarkable managed care penetration at 93.3%, substantially exceeding the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong program infrastructure and care coordination capabilities that position the state well for cost management and quality improvements.

However, Texas faces persistent quality challenges with a score of 62/100, ranking 30th nationally and falling below the national average of 65.6. While this "moderate" tier performance exceeds bottom-tier southern peers like Arkansas (54) and South Carolina (53), it lags behind expansion states and represents a critical area for improvement given the program's scale and managed care sophistication. The state's $12.0 billion total expenditure reflects efficient per-capita spending relative to peer large states, but the quality-cost balance requires attention. Key monitoring priorities include whether Texas can leverage its high managed care penetration to drive quality improvements, how enrollment stabilizes post-unwinding compared to expansion states like North Carolina (+0.5%), and whether ongoing federal regulatory activities around provider enrollment moratoria will impact network adequacy in this large, geographically diverse non-expansion state.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,869, indicating potential cost efficiency issues.

alerts.title: Michigan Enrollment Decline Exceeds 10% Threshold
alerts.description: Michigan experienced a -7.6% year-over-year enrollment decline, approaching the critical 10% threshold and indicating potential access issues.

alerts.title: Bottom 5 States Show Critical Quality Performance Gap
alerts.description: Five states (AR, SC, AL, OK, MS) scored below 55 on quality measures, with Mississippi at only 48 compared to the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Florida Shows High Efficiency with Low Per-Enrollee Costs
alerts.description: Florida maintains per-enrollee spending of $2,031 (29% below national average) while serving 3.6 million enrollees, demonstrating potential cost efficiency.

alerts.title: Nationwide Enrollment Moratorium Announced by CMS
alerts.description: Federal Register shows CMS announced temporary nationwide enrollment moratoriums affecting Medicare and Medicaid programs.

executiveInsights.title: CMS announces nationwide enrollment moratorium affecting Texas operations
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows CMS has announced a nationwide temporary moratorium on Medicaid enrollment. This regulatory action directly impacts Texas Medicaid operations and enrollment processing.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment freezes fundamentally alter system requirements, workflows, and processing volumes that Accenture manages for HHSC. This requires immediate operational adjustments and client communication.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag CMS enrollment moratorium to HHSC leadership immediately

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid enrollment declining 3.1% YoY with 4M+ current enrollees
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment stands at 4,032,864 with a -3.1% year-over-year decline. This matches the national trend but represents significant volume changes in system processing requirements.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Declining enrollment affects transaction volumes, system capacity planning, and potentially contract economics based on volume-dependent pricing models with HHSC.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review capacity planning assumptions with enrollment trend analysis

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score at 62/100 ranks #30, below national average of 65.6
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid quality performance sits at 62/100, ranking 30th nationally and falling short of the 65.6 national average. This places Texas in the 'moderate' performance tier.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality scores can trigger federal scrutiny, corrective action plans, and potential funding impacts. This creates opportunities for Accenture to propose quality improvement initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Develop quality improvement proposal targeting Texas ranking improvement

executiveInsights.title: CMS introduces new HCBS quality measures for 2028 implementation
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register announces new Medicaid Home and Community-Based Services quality measure set for 2028. This represents upcoming regulatory requirements for state Medicaid programs.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: New quality measures will require system modifications, reporting capabilities, and compliance workflows that Accenture must prepare to implement for Texas HHSC.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review 2028 HCBS quality measures for Texas system impact assessment

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs at $2,988 exceed national average by $119
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $2,988 per Medicaid enrollee compared to the national average of $2,868, representing a $119 premium. Total Texas expenditures reach $12.1 billion annually.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher than average costs suggest potential efficiency opportunities and cost management initiatives that Accenture could propose to help HHSC optimize spending.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Analyze Texas cost drivers versus lower-cost state benchmarks

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline acceleration
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropping 3.1% YoY creates budget pressures and potential service delivery disruptions.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop predictive analytics capabilities to help HHSC optimize resource allocation amid declining enrollment.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality score improvement gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, creating regulatory pressure and improvement opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality improvement roadmap targeting top 15 performance to differentiate Accenture's value proposition.

riskOpportunity.label: HCBS quality measures expansion
riskOpportunity.description: New 2028 HCBS quality measure requirements create compliance challenges and transformation opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position as lead implementer for HCBS quality transformation by developing specialized measurement and reporting capabilities.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete cycle
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration signals potential MCO contract renewals creating competitive procurement opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal actively and prepare strategic partnerships with MCOs for upcoming recompetes.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider enrollment moratorium
riskOpportunity.description: Nationwide temporary moratorium on provider enrollment creates network adequacy challenges and administrative complexity.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop alternative provider network strategies and enhanced screening capabilities to navigate moratorium constraints.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG oversight intensification
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG oversight creates compliance risks but positions quality-focused vendors favorably for future procurements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen compliance frameworks and audit readiness to differentiate from competitors during heightened scrutiny periods.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency pressure mounting
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee costs at $2,988 above FL ($2,031) but below national average creates optimization pressure and opportunity.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop cost reduction roadmap targeting 10-15% savings through automation and process optimization initiatives.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on quality measures, ranking 30th nationally in moderate tier.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurements
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two active procurement opportunities tracked through Texas HHSC and ESBD portals.

pulseMetrics.tooltip: Ten federal regulatory and OIG oversight items tracked across Medicaid programs.
